input.settingPrompt: A flooded crypt beneath a drowned cathedral, claimed by a hooded cult of tide-worshippers who tend its altars and drowned dead.

keyLocations.name: The Drowned Nave
keyLocations.description: the cathedral's collapsed main hall, ankle-deep in black water

keyLocations.name: The Reliquary Vault
keyLocations.description: a sealed vault rumored to hold a tide-saint's bones

keyLocations.name: The Surface Stair
keyLocations.description: the long spiral stair back up to the living world

identity.locationType: ceremonial tide-shrine and antechamber to the saint's reliquary
identity.characteristics: A wide, low-vaulted hall of salt-eaten basalt where the hooded cult tends a central altar and lays out its drowned dead in flanking biers; everything weeps brine and the air tastes of cold seawater.
identity.tone: solemn, reverent, drowned and funereal, heavy with ritual and damp silence

floor.texture: uneven slabs slick with brine, grouted with black silt and crusted at the edges with pale barnacles, a thin sheet of standing tidewater mirroring the candlelight

ceiling.texture: low groined vaults furred with mineral drip-stone and trailing strands of dead kelp, weeping slow beads of seawater

back.texture: dark stone carved with concentric tide-rings, streaked white with salt rime and veined by trickling water

front.texture: salt-rimed stone broken by the entry: a heavy door of black water-logged timber bound in corroded iron, swollen in its frame and standing ajar, with a greasy tide-line staining the stone to waist height on either side

left.material: dressed basalt blocks with shallow burial niches
left.texture: stone honeycombed with shrouded niches, hung with rotted votive ribbons and crusted barnacle clusters

right.material: dressed basalt blocks with shallow burial niches
right.texture: matching niches and salt-bleached stone, scrawled with the cult's looping tide-script in chalk and dried weed

objects.name: Tide-Altar
objects.description: A great slab altar of black basalt streaked with salt, raised on shallow worn steps, its surface cupped with channels that carry runoff brine back to the floor.

objects.name: Relief of the Drowned Saint
objects.description: A carved stone relief of the cult's tide-saint, hollow-eyed and crowned in coral, water seeping endlessly from the carved mouth.

objects.name: Bronze Brine-Basin
objects.description: A wide verdigris-green basin brimming with black brine, used to anoint offerings and the drowned dead.

objects.name: Coral-and-Bone Candelabrum (left)
objects.description: A tall candelabrum of fused coral and yellowed bone bearing guttering tallow candles, wax sheeting down it like dripped wax over reef.

objects.name: Coral-and-Bone Candelabrum (right)
objects.description: A twin candelabrum of fused coral and bone, mirroring its partner so the altar is framed in cold flame.

objects.name: Left Drowned-Bier
objects.description: A low stone bier bearing a shrouded corpse wrapped in kelp and weighted with stones, the linen darkened with seep.

objects.name: Right Drowned-Bier
objects.description: A matching stone bier with another shrouded form laid out for the tides, awaiting its turn at the altar.

objects.name: Tide-Censer
objects.description: A censer of green glass and coral suspended on a corroded chain, smoking faintly with salt-incense and dripping a slow thread of condensation.

objects.name: Kelp Prayer-Mat
objects.description: A woven mat of dried kelp-fiber, salt-stiffened and worn pale where countless knees have pressed before the altar.

objects.name: Grave-Urns
objects.description: A clustered set of barnacled clay urns holding the salt-ash and small tokens of the long-drowned dead.

objects.name: Litany Lectern
objects.description: A slender lectern grown of pale coral, holding open a swollen, water-buckled book of tide-litanies.

arrangementProse: The chamber opens through the swollen timber door set in the front wall, and the eye is drawn straight down a clear central aisle to the great Tide-Altar (obj_01), raised on shallow steps against the back wall, dead center. Set into the back wall directly above it is the carved Relief of the Drowned Saint (obj_02). On the altar's broad slab rests the bronze Brine-Basin (obj_03). Flanking the altar and standing against the back wall to either side are two candelabra of fused coral and bone (obj_04 to the left of the altar, obj_05 to the right), framing it in cold candlelight. A woven kelp Prayer-Mat (obj_09) lies centered on the floor at the foot of the altar where supplicants kneel. Along the left wall stands the left Drowned-Bier (obj_06) bearing a shrouded corpse, with a cluster of barnacled Grave-Urns (obj_10) gathered on the floor beside it against the same wall. Mirroring it along the right wall is the right Drowned-Bier (obj_07), beside which the coral Litany Lectern (obj_11) stands against the right wall. Overhead, the green-glass Tide-Censer (obj_08) hangs from the center of the vaulted ceiling above the aisle, dripping. The floor just inside the door and the line of sight to the altar are kept clear; all biers, urns, lectern, and candelabra are set back against the side and back walls.

placementNotes.note: Centered against the back wall, raised on shallow steps; the focal point at the end of the entry aisle.
Mounted on the back wall directly above the altar.
Resting on the broad slab of the altar.
Standing against the back wall to the left of the altar.
Standing against the back wall to the right of the altar.
Against the left wall, midway down the chamber.
Against the right wall, mirroring the left bier.
Hung from the center of the ceiling above the entry aisle.
Lies centered on the floor at the foot of the altar.
Clustered on the floor against the left wall beside the left bier.
Standing against the right wall beside the right bier.
